payload.description: Metro-North Penn Station Access will provide MNR New Haven Line customers with service into and out of Penn Station via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Includes four new ADA-accessible stations in the East Bronx, bridge rehabilitations, 19 miles of new and rehabilitated track work, new interlockings, signal/power/communication modernization.

tool_args.label: Force Account Personnel - OCS Portal Installation

payload.raw_value_text: Planning documents: 9 force account personnel required; Actual: average of 17 personnel required
payload.scope: Penn Station Access project - OCS portal installation activity
payload.description: Planned vs actual force account personnel required by Amtrak for OCS portal erection

evidence_refs.source_quote: Amtrak stated that 9 total force account personnel would be required to supervise and support contractor work to erect a steel beam that holds the catenary (OCS portal). On the ground, Amtrak has required an average of 17 personnel be present for this activity

tool_args.label: Monthly Construction Activities Planned vs Actual

payload.scope: Penn Station Access project
payload.description: Planned vs actual monthly construction activity completion rate

evidence_refs.source_quote: The initial schedule called for an average of 92 activities to be completed each month. The new project schedule shows only 53.

payload.claim_text: The Penn Station Access project faces delays in achieving revenue service and potential cost overruns, predominantly due to lack of cooperation and support by Amtrak.
payload.subject: Penn Station Access project
payload.description: Project delays and potential cost overruns attributed primarily to Amtrak's lack of cooperation and support

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Penn Station Access project faces delays in achieving revenue service and potential cost overruns, predominantly due to lack of cooperation and support by Amtrak. Decisive action is needed to get this project on track and deliver rail service to East Bronx residents.

payload.claim_text: Leggett interlocking provides Amtrak with greater operational flexibility and allows the project to work in constant cyclical long-term single-track outages to provide the contractor with greater access
payload.subject: Leggett Interlocking
payload.description: Purpose of the Leggett Interlocking installation

evidence_refs.source_quote: Leggett interlocking provides Amtrak with greater operational flexibility and allows the project to work in constant cyclical long-term single-track outages to provide the contractor with greater access

tool_args.label: Temporary Service Option for East Bronx

payload.claim_text: With Amtrak's agreement, it would be possible to begin temporary service starting in 2027 as originally planned. Work could be re-sequenced to deliver a robust limited service to Co-op City, Morris Park, and Parkchester-Van Nest stations in 2027 while the rest of the construction continues.
payload.subject: Penn Station Access
payload.description: Potential temporary service option for East Bronx residents while construction continues

evidence_refs.source_quote: it would be possible to begin temporary service starting in 2027 as originally planned

payload.claim_text: MTA made efforts to partner with Amtrak and the contractor to mitigate these delays. In particular, the MTA's team has worked with Amtrak to document and implement a Book of Rules, which helped to define and standardize Amtrak's force account support requirements and work rules, and implemented Design Partnering Workshops to speed up design reviews.
payload.subject: MTA
payload.description: Mitigation efforts undertaken by MTA to address project delays

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA made efforts to partner with Amtrak and the contractor to mitigate these delays

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Construction & Development's (C&D)
Metro-North Penn Station Access CBX001
As the owner of the right-of-way, Amtrak
MTA C&D awarded the Design-Build (DB) contract to Halmar International, LLC/Railworks Joint Venture (HRJV)
October 2025 Capital Program Committee Independent Engineering Consultant Project Review
Michael Baker
Penn Station Access will provide Metro-North Railroad New Haven Line customers with service into and out of Penn Station
Original Forecast Substantial Completion March 2027
The PSA re-baseline schedule forecasts a revenue service/substantial completion date in March 2030
base contract Notice to Proceed (NTP) was issued on January 3, 2022
Leggett Interlocking was completed July 2025
In February 2025, the MTA Board approved the design, manufacturing, testing, and delivery of 13 Dual-Mode Locomotives
New Rochelle Yard Improvements option was awarded in December 2022

tool_args.label: IEC Recommendation: T&C Time in Re-Baseline

payload.claim_text: Based on lessons learned from the cutover of Leggett Interlocking, the IEC recommends MTA C&D ensure sufficient detail and time for Testing & Commissioning (T&C) activities are incorporated into the re-baseline schedule. Allotting adequate time to place each of the remaining interlocking into service reduces the risk of further critical path delay.
payload.subject: IEC / Michael Baker International
payload.description: IEC recommendation for the Penn Station Access re-baseline schedule

evidence_refs.source_quote: the IEC recommends MTA C&D ensure sufficient detail and time for Testing & Commissioning (T&C) activities are incorporated into the re-baseline schedule

payload.raw_value_text: 5 trains during each of the AM and PM peaks and 2 trains in reverse peak direction
payload.description: Proposed temporary service: 5 trains AM peak, 5 PM peak, 2 reverse peak, bi-directional hourly service 6AM-8:30PM weekdays outside peak
payload.scope: Penn Station Access temporary service option

evidence_refs.source_quote: The temporary service would include 5 trains during each of the AM and PM peaks and 2 trains in reverse peak direction to enable Bronx residents to commute to jobs in Westchester and Connecticut

tool_args.label: Three New Railroad Bridge Spans Completed

payload.description: Three new railroad bridge spans completed: Bronxdale Ave Bridge (tracks 2 & 4), Bridge River Bridge (track 1), and Eastchester Rd. Bridge (track 3). Pelham Lane Bridge foundations and piers complete.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Three new railroad bridge spans are completed: Bronxdale Ave Bridge (tracks 2 & 4), Bridge River Bridge (track 1), and Eastchester Rd. Bridge (track 3)

payload.description: Design, manufacturing, testing, and delivery of 13 Dual-Mode Locomotives with option for 2 additional, under separate project with Siemens Mobility, Inc. Battery-alternating current configuration replacing diesel engine with traction batteries.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the MTA Board approved the design, manufacturing, testing, and delivery of 13 Dual-Mode Locomotives and related equipment with an option to purchase two additional Dual-Mode Locomotives under a separate project with Siemens Mobility, Inc.
